heading: Borrowed Sources
fact: DPSP is derived from Ireland (which took it from Spain). Fundamental Duties are inspired by the former USSR Constitution.

heading: Non-Justiciable Nature
fact: Neither DPSPs nor Fundamental Duties are directly enforceable by courts, but courts use them to interpret constitutional validity of laws.

heading: Swaran Singh Committee
fact: Recommended 8 Fundamental Duties in 1976. The 42nd CAA enacted 10 duties; the 11th duty was added in 2002.

heading: Novel Features
fact: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described the Directive Principles of State Policy as the 'Novel Features' of the Indian Constitution.
